title: Robust Gibbs Free Energy Minimization for Chemical Equilibrium Calculations with Cubic Equations of State
novelty_statement: Integration of Armijo backtracking line search and Tikhonov regularization into a constrained Gibbs free energy minimization algorithm using Lagrange multipliers and cubic equations of state. The enhancements guarantee monotonic decrease of the objective function and well-conditioned Newton steps, providing global convergence properties that fixed-damping approaches lack. Systematic comparison on combustion, acid gas, and synthesis reaction systems demonstrates improved robustness.

research_questions.question: Does an Armijo backtracking line search improve convergence reliability of the Lagrangian Newton-Raphson method for Gibbs minimization compared to fixed-step damping?
research_questions.hypothesis: The Armijo condition ensures monotonic decrease of the total Gibbs energy at each step, preventing divergence on difficult cases where fixed damping may overshoot or undershoot.
research_questions.method: Paired comparison on 6 chemical systems (combustion, sulfur, ammonia synthesis, CO2 acid gas, H2/O2, multi-component) with convergence tracking.
research_questions.acceptance_criterion: Armijo variant converges on all cases where fixed damping converges, with equal or better mass balance accuracy.

research_questions.question: Does Tikhonov regularization of the KKT Jacobian improve robustness for near-singular systems?
research_questions.hypothesis: Adding tau*I to the Hessian block when the condition number exceeds a threshold prevents step divergence on ill-conditioned systems while introducing negligible perturbation to well-conditioned ones.
research_questions.method: Condition number monitoring across iterations for all test systems, with and without regularization.
research_questions.acceptance_criterion: Regularized solver handles all test cases with condition numbers tracked and no convergence failures due to singularity.

research_questions.question: What is the computational overhead of the proposed enhancements?
research_questions.hypothesis: The overhead is small (<15% CPU time increase) because Armijo requires only additional objective function evaluations (no new Jacobian factorizations), and regularization is a diagonal perturbation.
research_questions.method: Timing comparison with 5 repeated trials per case, reported as mean +/- std.
research_questions.acceptance_criterion: Mean CPU time increase < 20% for the enhanced algorithm.

workflow_log.notes: Implemented Armijo line search, Tikhonov regularization, and convergence diagnostics in GibbsReactor.java. All 23 tests pass.
